nameMeanings.name: Browne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably also an Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Braune   or   Braun  .
Irish: phonetic Anglicization of Mac an Bhreitheamhnaigh; see Breheny .

variants.name: Brown
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: generally a nickname referring to the color of the hair or complexion, Middle English br(o)un, from Old English brūn or Old French brun. This word is occasionally found in Old French, Middle English and Old Norse as a personal name or byname (Middle English personal name Brun, Broun, ancient Germanic Bruno, Old English Brūn, or possibly Old Norse Brúnn or Brúni). Brun- was also an ancient Germanic name-forming element. Some instances of Old English Brūn as a personal name may therefore be short forms of compound names such as Brūngar, Brūnwine, etc. As a Scottish and Irish name, it sometimes represents a translation of Gaelic Donn (see below). Brown (including in the senses below) is the fourth most frequent surname in the US. It is also very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below).
Irish and Scottish: adopted for Ó Duinn (see Dunn ) or for any of the many Irish and Scottish Gaelic names containing the element donn ‘brown-haired’ (also meaning ‘chieftain’), for example Donahue .
Irish: phonetic Anglicization of Mac an Bhreitheamhnaigh; see Breheny .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William Browne was born in 1470, in Surrey, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Brown Jr, was 21 and his mother, Grace Pinchbeck, was 13. He had at least 5 sons and 2 daughters with Mary Margaret Mallet. He died in 1506, in England, at the age of 36, and was buried in City of London, England, United Kingdom.

ko.lifeSummary: William Browne(이)가 1470년 영국 잉글랜드 수리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Brown Jr(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Grace Pinchbeck(은)는 13세였다. 그는 Mary Margaret Mallet(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1506년 영국에서 36세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 시티 프 런던에 묻혔다.
